From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

In mathematics, the **Wiener–Wintner theorem**, named after [Norbert Wiener](/wiki/Norbert_Wiener "Norbert Wiener") and [Aurel Wintner](/wiki/Aurel_Wintner "Aurel Wintner"), is a strengthening of the [ergodic theorem](/wiki/Ergodic_theorem "Ergodic theorem"), proved by [Wiener](/wiki/Norbert_Wiener "Norbert Wiener") and Wintner ([1941](#CITEREFWienerWintner1941)).

## Statement

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wiener%E2%80%93Wintner_theorem&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Statement")]

Suppose that *τ* is a measure-preserving transformation of a measure space *S* with finite measure. If *f* is a real-valued integrable function on *S* then the Wiener–Wintner theorem states that there is a measure 0 set *E* such that the average

:   ![{\displaystyle \lim _{\ell \rightarrow \infty }{\frac {1}{2\ell +1}}\sum _{j=-\ell }^{\ell }e^{ij\lambda }f(\tau ^{j}P)}](https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/58e31bd71c04f68a533389b861bd5299eca3daee)

exists for all real λ and for all *P* not in *E*.

The special case for *λ* = 0 is essentially the [Birkhoff ergodic theorem](/wiki/Birkhoff_ergodic_theorem "Birkhoff ergodic theorem"), from which the existence of a suitable measure 0 set *E* for any fixed *λ*, or any countable set of values *λ*, immediately follows. The point of the Wiener–Wintner theorem is that one can choose the measure 0 exceptional set *E* to be independent of *λ*.

This theorem was even much more generalized by
the Return Times Theorem.

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wiener%E2%80%93Wintner_theorem&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: References")]

* [Assani, I.](/wiki/Idris_Assani "Idris Assani") (2001) [1994], ["Wiener–Wintner theorem"](https://www.encyclopediaofmath.org/index.php?title=Wiener–Wintner_theorem), *[Encyclopedia of Mathematics](/wiki/Encyclopedia_of_Mathematics "Encyclopedia of Mathematics")*, [EMS Press](/wiki/European_Mathematical_Society "European Mathematical Society")
* Wiener, Norbert; Wintner, Aurel (1941), "Harmonic analysis and ergodic theory", *[American Journal of Mathematics](/wiki/American_Journal_of_Mathematics "American Journal of Mathematics")*, **63** (2): 415–426, [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.2307/2371534](https://doi.org/10.2307%2F2371534), [ISSN](/wiki/ISSN_(identifier) "ISSN (identifier)") [0002-9327](https://search.worldcat.org/issn/0002-9327), [JSTOR](/wiki/JSTOR_(identifier) "JSTOR (identifier)") [2371534](https://www.jstor.org/stable/2371534), [MR](/wiki/MR_(identifier) "MR (identifier)") [0004098](https://mathscinet.ams.org/mathscinet-getitem?mr=0004098)
